TITLE: feat: SAM 3 video object tracking
LABELS: roadmap, type:feat, size:XL, phase:D-sam3
---
## Context

With C1 (videos as lazily-decoded frame slices) and D2 (SAM 3 loaded and wired into the app) in place, the flagship video feature becomes possible: select an object's mask on one frame and have SAM 3 track it across the video, writing a per-frame annotation for every frame it appears in. Tracking must run forward AND backward from the seed frame, commit through the standard annotation path (so results are saved, exported, and undoable like any other annotation), flag low-confidence frames for human review through the existing temp-annotation pipeline, and paint its coverage on the C2 timeline.

Two design facts are **verify-first** items, not assumptions: (a) undo history is per-image-key, so a 200-frame track is 200 separate undo entries — the UX consequence must be decided explicitly; (b) SAM 3's video-predictor memory behaviour on long videos must be measured before choosing whole-video vs chunked propagation.

## Current state

- Undo model: `controllers/annotation_history.py` — snapshots are kept **per image key** (`current_slice or image_file_name`; docstring lines 10-13, `record` at 45-57). `AnnotationController.record_history(key=None)` (controllers/annotation_controller.py:296-314) accepts an explicit key for off-screen writes; `undo()`/`redo()` (388-408) operate ONLY on the currently displayed key. Consequence: Ctrl+Z after a track undoes one frame, not the track.
- Off-screen commit pattern to copy: `DINOController._commit_dino_results` (controllers/dino_controller.py:495-538) — `record_history(image_name)` first (508), then write to `image_label.annotations` if current else `all_annotations[image_name]` (511-515), per-class `number` assignment (524-525), `save_current_annotations` + canvas update if current (536-538).
- Review pipeline for uncertain frames: `dino_batch_results[image_key]` + `_store_dino_batch_results` (540-552), `_show_dino_batch_review` (554-577), `_navigate_to_image_or_slice` (579-611 — already resolves slice/frame keys), `_refresh_dino_temp_for_current` (613-636), accept/reject (638-680).
- Video access: `core/video_handler.py` (C1) — `get_frame(idx)` lazy decode with LRU, `frame_key`/`parse_frame_index`; handlers in `mw.video_handlers[base]`. Timeline: `widgets/video_timeline.py` (C2) — `set_annotated_frames(...)`, marker strip.
- SAM 3 backend: `inference/sam3_utils.py` (D2) — `_run_sync` threading (ADR-013, sam_utils.py:244-287), lazy load, `_mask_to_polygon` conversion (sam_utils.py:114-130).
- Canvas selection: a single selected annotation is exposed via `image_label` selection state (`highlighted_annotations`; single-shape resolution pattern `_single_selected_shape` per ADR-023) — the seed mask comes from there.
- Threading rule: inference only from the GUI thread through `_run_sync` (tripwire sam_utils.py:262-268); `InferenceBusyError` (222) on re-entry.
- No tracking code exists anywhere in `src/` today.

## Proposed design

**Backend — extend `inference/sam3_utils.py`:**
- `track(self, base_name, frame_provider, seed_idx, seed_polygon, direction, on_frame, should_cancel) -> None` where `frame_provider(idx) -> np.ndarray | None` wraps `VideoHandler.get_frame` (+ `_qimage_to_numpy`), `direction` is `"forward" | "backward"`, and `on_frame(idx, result)` receives `{"segmentation": [...], "score": float}` or `None` per processed frame. Implement per the D1 ADR's video API; if the predictor only propagates forward, backward = feed frames in reverse index order. Wrap the whole propagation in ONE `_run_sync` call with `on_frame` results accumulated and returned (callbacks cannot touch Qt from the worker thread — return a list `[(idx, result), ...]` instead of calling back cross-thread; keep the `on_frame` name only if invoked after `_run_sync` returns).
- **Chunking (verify-first)**: first implementation step is a scratch measurement of predictor memory vs frames processed (see step 2). If state grows with video length, propagate in chunks of N frames (default ~100, re-seeding each chunk from the last accepted mask) and `reset`/re-init the predictor between chunks.

**Controller — new `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/tracking_controller.py`** (`TrackingController(QObject)`, `self.mw` pattern like every other controller; registered in `ImageAnnotator.__init__` next to `dino_controller`, annotator_window.py:124-129):
- `can_track()` — current image `is_video`, SAM 3 loaded, exactly one annotation selected with a `"segmentation"` key (pose instances excluded — no `segmentation` is the keypoint discriminator).
- `run_tracking()` — confirm dialog (frame range = whole video, both directions; confidence threshold spinbox, default from the D1 ADR, e.g. 0.5); `QProgressDialog` with cancel; call `sam3_utils.track` backward then forward; then apply results:
  - `score >= threshold` → commit via a `_commit_tracked_result(frame_name, polygon, score, class_name)` that mirrors `_commit_dino_results` exactly: `record_history(frame_name)` first, `"source": "sam3-track"`, per-class `number`, write to `all_annotations[frame_name]` (or `image_label.annotations` when current);
  - `score < threshold` (and non-None) → `dino_batch_results[frame_name] = [temp-shaped entry with "source": "sam3"]` so the EXISTING review pipeline handles it; after tracking, offer "Review N uncertain frames now?" → `_show_dino_batch_review()`;
  - `None` (object absent) → nothing written.
  - Finish: `update_annotation_list`, `update_slice_list_colors`, `update_video_timeline`, `auto_save` (once, at the end — not per frame).
- **Rollback affordance** (the undo-granularity decision): because Ctrl+Z is per-frame, keep the full result map `{frame_name: committed}` for the last run and offer "Undo last track" (button in the dialog summary or Tools menu) that, per affected frame, calls `record_history(frame_name)` then removes exactly the annotations tagged with this run's id (`"track_run": <uuid>` field on each committed dict — serializes fine, ignored by exporters). Document in the ADR that per-frame Ctrl+Z remains the primitive and this is a bulk convenience.
- UI entry: "Track Selected Object…" — a button next to the Detect buttons region or a Tools-menu action (menu pattern ui/menu_bar.py:111-158); enabled-state driven by `can_track()` re-evaluated on selection change.

**Timeline (C2 widget reuse):** extend `VideoTimeline` marks API to states — `set_frame_states(dict[int, str])` with `"annotated" | "tracked" | "needs_review"`, painted as contiguous coloured segments (runs of equal state) using distinct palette-derived colours (`highlight`, a desaturated variant, and `palette().brightText()`/warning tone — no hex literals). `needs_review` frames derive from `dino_batch_results` keys; clicking the timeline on one navigates there and the existing `_refresh_dino_temp_for_current` shows the pending mask.

## Implementation steps

1. Branch `feature/sam3-video-tracking`.
2. **Verify-first spike (in-branch, scratch scripts not committed)**: (a) measure SAM 3 video-predictor memory growth over 500+ frames; decide whole-video vs chunked and record the numbers; (b) confirm backward propagation support. Write both findings into the ADR (below) before building on them.
3. Implement `sam3_utils.track` (+ chunking if required) with the result-list-through-`_run_sync` shape; unit-test with the predictor monkeypatched.
4. Implement `TrackingController` (`can_track`, `run_tracking`, `_commit_tracked_result`, uncertain-frame routing, run-id rollback) and register it on the window.
5. UI entry point + enabled-state wiring; progress dialog with responsive cancel (check `should_cancel` between frames/chunks — results already produced are still applied, matching `run_dino_detection_batch`'s cancel semantics at dino_controller.py:405-406).
6. Extend `VideoTimeline` with `set_frame_states` + segment painting; wire `needs_review` from `dino_batch_results`; keep `set_annotated_frames` delegating to it for C2 compatibility.
7. Manual golden path: annotate an object on frame 10 of a ~100-frame clip, select it, Track → progress → summary "N committed, M for review"; scrub the timeline (segments visible); review an uncertain frame with Enter/Escape; Ctrl+Z on one frame removes only that frame's tracked mask; "Undo last track" clears the run; save/reload → tracked annotations persist; export → per-frame labels present.
8. Update docs, run the senior-reviewer agent, open PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] With a video frame displayed and exactly one mask selected, tracking runs forward AND backward from the seed frame; entry point disabled otherwise (and for pose instances).
- [ ] High-confidence frames get committed annotations (`source: "sam3-track"`, `track_run` id) written through `record_history` + the standard save path; visible per frame, persisted in `.iap`, included in exports.
- [ ] Frames below the confidence threshold land in `dino_batch_results` and are reviewable with the EXISTING Enter/Escape flow, including navigation between uncertain frames; no new review UI.
- [ ] Frames where the object is absent get no annotation.
- [ ] Timeline shows coloured segments distinguishing annotated / tracked / needs-review, correct in light AND dark mode, updating live as reviews are accepted.
- [ ] Ctrl+Z on a tracked frame undoes that frame only; "Undo last track" removes the whole run (both behaviours documented in the ADR).
- [ ] Cancel mid-track keeps already-processed frames and stops cleanly; UI stays responsive throughout (progress repaints, cancel clickable — ADR-013).
- [ ] Memory bounded on long videos per the measured strategy (chunk size documented); no unbounded predictor state growth.
- [ ] Full test suite green; C1/C2/D2 behaviours unchanged.

## Test plan

- `tests/unit/test_sam3_tracking.py`: monkeypatch the predictor inside `sam3_utils.track` with a fake yielding deterministic masks/scores per index; assert forward+backward index coverage, `None` handling, cancel-early stops iteration, and (if chunked) the fake sees re-seeding at chunk boundaries.
- `tests/integration/test_video_tracking.py` — window fixture (`tests/integration/test_image_filter_wiring.py:23-29`) + the C1 MJPG/AVI synthetic-video helper; stub `window.sam3_utils` so `track` returns a canned `[(idx, result)]` list with scores straddling the threshold:
  - run `tracking_controller.run_tracking()` with dialogs monkeypatched → high-score frames appear in `all_annotations[frame_key]` with `source == "sam3-track"` and a shared `track_run` id; low-score frames appear in `window.dino_batch_results` and NOT in `all_annotations`;
  - navigate to the seed frame's neighbour and `annotation_controller.undo()` → only that frame's annotation removed (per-key granularity regression);
  - "Undo last track" → all run-tagged annotations gone, other pre-existing annotations intact;
  - accept one uncertain frame via `accept_dino_results()` → committed and removed from `dino_batch_results`;
  - save/reload project round-trip keeps tracked annotations (reuse the C1 roundtrip pattern).
- `tests/unit/test_video_timeline.py` — extend with `set_frame_states` segment-run computation asserts (pure logic, no painting asserts).
- Run: `pytest tests/unit/test_sam3_tracking.py tests/integration/test_video_tracking.py tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`, then `pytest tests/ -v`.
- Manual (unmockable): real SAM 3 track on a real clip per the CLAUDE.md inference checklist — model loads end-to-end, masks track plausibly, UI responsive, cancel works; watch process RSS across a long clip to validate the chunking decision.

## Docs to update

- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — new ADR "SAM 3 video tracking: standard commit path, per-frame undo + run rollback, chunked propagation" recording BOTH verify-first findings (memory numbers, backward support) and the undo-granularity decision. Use the next free ADR number (several will have been taken by C1/D1/D2 — read the file's current max, don't assume).
- `docs/05_building_block_view.md` — add `TrackingController`; update controller count/table.
- `docs/06_runtime_view.md` — "Track object across video" workflow (seed → propagate → commit/review split → timeline).
- `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` — extend "DINO Temp Annotations" (tracking is a third producer feeding `dino_batch_results`) and the C1 video section (frame_provider contract).
- `docs/12_glossary.md` — "Track run", "Seed frame".
- CLAUDE.md — delete this issue's backlog row in the same PR if present.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Undo/redo snapshot model** (CLAUDE.md, verbatim-ish): "**Snapshot** the whole per-image annotation dict… `record_history()` is the choke-point, called **before** each synchronous mutation." Never write to `all_annotations[frame_key]` without `record_history(frame_key)` first; never hook `save_current_annotations` for history (it also fires on navigation).
- **Temp-annotation single field** (verbatim): "`image_label.temp_annotations` is a single field, **not** per-image — must be re-synced from `dino_batch_results` on every image/slice switch via `_refresh_dino_temp_for_current`." Uncertain tracked frames must live in `dino_batch_results`; do not attach them to `temp_annotations` for frames that aren't on screen.
- **Threading (ADR-013)**: the whole propagation runs inside ONE `_run_sync`; the worker must not touch Qt objects — pass numpy frames in via the provider (called on the worker is NOT allowed if it reads QImages; pre-fetch to numpy on the GUI side per chunk, or make the provider decode with cv2 directly, bypassing QImage). `VideoHandler`'s `cv2.VideoCapture` is not thread-safe with concurrent GUI-side scrubbing — either block navigation during tracking (progress dialog is modal — `WindowModal` like dino_controller.py:401 — which already prevents it) or give the tracker its own capture instance. State the choice in the ADR.
- **Event-loop re-entrancy**: `_run_sync` pumps events — the modal progress dialog is the guard against the user switching images mid-track; do not weaken it to non-modal without adding an explicit busy gate. Catch `InferenceBusyError` and abort cleanly.
- **Keypoint discriminator** (verbatim-ish): "a pose instance… has **no `segmentation` key** — that absence is the discriminator." `can_track` must require `"segmentation" in ann`; also never emit a `segmentation: None` key on any dict — several existence-only `"segmentation" in annotation` checks elsewhere would misfire.
- **Dark mode** (verbatim): "No hardcoded `background:` / `color:` in widget `setStyleSheet(...)`" — segment colours from palette roles only.
- **Auto-save cost**: one `auto_save()` at the end of the run, not per committed frame — per-frame saves rewrite the whole `.iap` N times (project_controller.py:391-523 serialises everything each call).
- **Exact-key matching** (verbatim): "Exact-key match first, substring fallback only — `\"bee.jpg\" in \"honeybee.jpg\"` is True." Frame-key lookups in the run map and rollback must be exact.
- **GPU memory**: tracking + a resident SAM 2/DINO can OOM; reuse the `unload()` recipe (sam_utils.py:368-406) and surface OOM as a dialog, not a silent stop.

## Dependencies

- Blocked by {{C1}} (video frames + lazy decode) and {{D2}} (SAM 3 backend + review-pipeline plumbing).
- Recommended, not blocking: {{C2}} — timeline segments ship only if the widget exists; without it, skip step 6 and file a follow-up (all other AC stand alone).
- Recommended, not blocking: {{B5}} (bounded slice memory) for very long videos.

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
